payload.text:
Create a premium, ultra-smooth portfolio website for Pranjal Kumar, professionally known as PRAKU.
PRAKU is a Video Editor, Graphic Designer, and Photographer.
The website must feel cinematic, modern, elegant, and extremely smooth in loading and transitions.
🎨 Brand Identity
Brand Name: PRAKU
Full Name: Pranjal Kumar
Tagline: Crafting Visual Stories with Precision
Email: pk4470588@gmail.com
Style tone:
Premium
Clean
Elegant
High-end creative studio vibe
Smooth, professional, minimal luxury
🚀 UI & Performance Focus (Very Important)
Ultra smooth loading (no lag)
Lightweight animations
Lazy loading images
Optimized assets
60fps animations
Smooth scroll with inertia
Soft fade transitions between sections
No heavy clutter
Make it feel like Apple-level smoothness.
🧊 Design Style
Glass morphism UI (blurred background panels)
Subtle 3D elements
Floating layered cards
Soft glow lighting
Smooth parallax scrolling
Micro-interactions on hover
Premium typography (bold headings + clean body font)
🎨 Premium Color System
Main Palette (Signature Look)
Deep Midnight Blue #0F172A
Royal Purple Gradient #6D28D9 → #9333EA
Neon Cyan Accent #22D3EE
Frosted Glass White (with blur transparency)
Alternative Mode
Matte Black #0B0B0F
Soft Gold Accent #D4AF37
Electric Blue Highlight #3B82F6
🌗 Theme Modes (Toggle Required)
Create a smooth toggle system for:
Light Mode
Dark Mode
Glass Mode (High transparency + blur)
Premium Glow Mode (Subtle animated gradient + glow effects)
Theme switch must animate smoothly without reload.
🏠 Hero Section
Large cinematic intro:
Headline: “PRAKU – Visual Excellence Redefined”
Subheading: Video Editor | Graphic Designer | Photographer
Include:
Smooth fade-in animation
Floating 3D camera or editing timeline element
CTA Buttons:
View Work
Contact Me
Buttons must have:
Glow on hover
Smooth scaling
Magnetic effect
🎬 Projects Section
Modern grid layout with smooth hover animation.
Each project card must include:
Thumbnail image
Title
Category (Video / Graphic / Photo)
Smooth hover zoom
Click to open modal preview
⚙️ Project Management Feature (Important)
Add admin panel functionality:
Upload new project
Add thumbnail
Add title & description
Delete project
Edit project
Drag and reorder projects
Must be structured so backend can be connected later.
👤 About Section
Glass card layout.
Include:
Photo placeholder
Short professional bio
Animated skill bars:
Video Editing
Motion Graphics
Branding Design
Photography
Color Grading
📩 Contact Section
Elegant glass contact form:
Fields:
Name
Email
Message
Send to: pk4470588@gmail.com
Smooth submit animation + success confirmation effect.
🧠 Extra Premium Details
Custom animated cursor
Soft background ambient lighting
Smooth page loader animation
Scroll-triggered animations
Subtle 3D tilt effect on cards
SEO optimized
Fully responsive
Mobile-first smooth design

**Defining the Core Need**

I'm now zeroing in on the essential requirements, specifically that this website needs to showcase Pranjal Kumar's skills as a video editor, graphic designer, and photographer in a cinematic, modern style. This initial analysis helps me set the stage for later feature prioritization.


**Visualizing the Ideal Style**

I'm now picturing a website with an elegant, ultra-smooth, high-end creative studio vibe. I'm aiming for Apple-level smoothness, utilizing glassmorphism, subtle 3D effects, and parallax scrolling. I've also defined the brand colors: Midnight Blue, Royal Purple, Neon Cyan, and Frosted Glass, with a Matte Black and Soft Gold alternative. I'm considering light, dark, glass, and glow theme toggles. I plan for a cinematic hero intro, a project grid with hover effects and modal previews, an about section with skill bars, and a functional contact form. The admin system will allow for project management via add/edit/delete/reorder. I'm leaning toward React, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, and Lucide React for the tech stack. I'm planning to deconstruct the existing app's content and structure.


**Outlining the Implementation Path**

I'm solidifying the implementation steps. I will retain the React/Tailwind/Framer Motion foundation. I've sketched out the initial steps for setting up themes in App.tsx using a state/context, and making sure the root container is responsive to the selected theme. I'm focusing next on a floating glass navbar for navigation. Then, I plan the hero section, with large typography, "PRAKU" branding, and an animated gradient background for performance, and a 3D-ish text effect using Framer Motion. I'll integrate the theme switcher into the navbar. Finally, I will structure the projects section, along with the admin section.


**Detailing the Project Structure**

I'm now outlining key components. I am prioritizing a drag-and-drop reordering system. I am moving on to designing the 'About' and 'Contact' sections, with glass cards and animated skill bars, and a validation-capable glass form, respectively. Finally, I will add custom cursor implementation and smooth scrolling. I am defining a simple data structure for projects. I've begun to define the CSS variables and theme configurations.


**Enhancing the Aesthetic**

I'm now integrating `AnimatePresence` for smoother transitions and modals, alongside `whileHover={{ scale: 1.05 }}` for interactive cards. I am utilizing backdrop-blur liberally for a truly cinematic aesthetic. I've updated the `index.html` file with font updates, considering 'Outfit' for headings to get a more creative vibe, and I have started the file-by-file changes.


**Refining the Admin Interface**

I'm currently focused on the admin panel, implementing an "Edit Mode" toggle with intuitive "Add Project" and "Delete/Edit" buttons that appear when active. I'll use Framer Motion's `Reorder.Group` for seamless project reordering functionality. I am thinking about how the theme logic will affect the layout of the admin panel.


**Finalizing the File Structure**

I've just completed a final pass over the component breakdown. I've streamlined the file structure, merging some smaller components into `Sections.tsx` to simplify the project. I renamed `QuantumScene.tsx` to `Background3D.tsx`, aiming for a more relevant and creative studio aesthetic. I'm focusing next on implementing the changes to CSS variables for the cursor and specific glow effects.

Here is the implementation of the premium portfolio website for PRAKU. I have completely redesigned the application to match the "Apple-level smoothness," cinematic vibe, and requested feature set (Admin panel, Themes, Glassmorphism).

### Changes Overview:

1.  **`index.html`**: Added 'Outfit' font for a modern, tech-creative look.
2.  **`types.ts`**: Defined interfaces for Projects and Theme configurations.
3.  **`index.css`**: Added advanced glassmorphism utilities, custom cursor styles, and smooth scroll behaviors.
4.  **`components/Visuals.tsx` (formerly QuantumScene)**: Created a subtle, floating "Glass Lenses" 3D background that reacts to the mouse, fitting for a photographer/designer.
5.  **`components/ProjectSystem.tsx`**: A comprehensive component containing:
    *   The Portfolio Grid with filtering.
    *   **Admin Mode**: Functionality to Add, Edit, Delete, and Reorder projects (using `framer-motion` layout animations).
    *   Image handling and form inputs.
6.  **`components/Sections.tsx`**: Contains the Hero, About (with animated skill bars), and Contact sections using high-end glass UI.
7.  **`App.tsx`**: The main orchestrator handling:
    *   Theme Switching (Midnight, Light, Glass, Glow).
    *   Custom Magnetic Cursor.
    *   Smooth Navigation.

entries.description: Add Outfit font and update title.
New file for Project Portfolio and Admin System.
Remove unused old scene (replaced by Visuals.tsx).
Main application with Theme management and Navigation.

payload.text: When in admin mode, add an 'Add Video' button next to the 'video URL' input field in the 'New Project' form. This button should trigger a file upload interface, allowing users to select an video from their device.

**Analyzing Form Integration** I'm currently focused on integrating the "Add Video" button into the "New Project" form within the admin interface. My analysis involves examining the existing `ProjectSystem.tsx` file to understand how the "Image URL" field is currently handled and adapting that to include a "Video URL" field and its associated button. The goal is a seamless addition, enhancing the form's functionality for video uploads. **Refining Input Field Logic** I'm now diving deeper into how the `Project` type, specifically the `image` property in the `ProjectSystem.tsx` file, handles image uploads. My focus is expanding this to allow for a 'Video URL' field in the "New Project" form. The plan is to create a parallel field to 'Image URL', incorporating a corresponding button to handle video integration similarly to image uploads. The critical aspect is to ensure the new video field works smoothly. **Considering Video File Handling** I'm now grappling with the implications of video file uploads within the existing `Project` structure. I've realized the `Project` interface currently only includes an `image` field, not a `video` field. The system seems URL-centric, but user input suggests an 'Add Video' feature, hinting at potential file upload integration. I'm considering whether to adapt the existing `image` field to accommodate video URLs or introduce a completely new `video` field alongside. The key consideration is how to best handle the potential video file upload logic: data URLs or simulating it in frontend. **Clarifying Media Field Implementation** I'm now clarifying the approach to the 'Media URL' field. The user's request, specifically "next to the 'video URL' input field," suggests several possibilities. I'm leaning toward renaming the existing "Image URL" field to "Media URL (Image/Video)" and adapting its upload button to handle both image and video URLs, or creating a specific Video URL field. The current implementation uses an `img` tag which means that I will need to update the `Project` interface to support either checking the file extension or MIME type, or distinguishing between image and video. I will also need to update how it's displayed, as video playback requires a `

To satisfy your request, I will update `components/ProjectSystem.tsx` to include a file upload button next to the URL input field in the project editing form. I have also enhanced the component to render `<video>` elements if the media source is a video file or data URL, ensuring a fully functional media experience.

### Changes Implemented:
1.  **Added File Upload:** Added a hidden file input and an "Upload" button (using the `Upload` icon) next to the "Image / Video URL" input field.
2.  **Handle Uploads:** Implemented `handleFileUpload` to read selected files (images or videos) as Data URLs (base64) and populate the form field.
3.  **Video Rendering:** Added an `isVideo` helper function to detect video URLs. Updated the Grid View to play muted previews on hover, and the Modal View to show full video controls.
4.  **UI Updates:** Renamed the input label to "Image / Video URL" for clarity.

entries.description: Add video upload button to form and implement video rendering logic.

payload.text: Add a dynamic, animated background to the Hero section that subtly reacts to mouse movement, creating a more immersive and cinematic feel. Use a particle system or a fluid simulation effect. Category: UI/UX Improvement and buy default it should be in light mode and the all the users can upload their projects and photos videos of whatever they want to upload so add in the menu bar projects uploading and deleting option to upload their project

**Considering Hero Section Updates**

I'm currently focused on the hero section. My primary consideration is how to introduce a dynamic, animated background that reacts to user mouse movements. I'm exploring two main approaches: a particle system or a fluid simulation. I need to weigh the pros and cons of each, specifically concerning performance and visual impact. I'm leaning toward an interactive element that's visually compelling.


**Prioritizing User Accessibility**

I'm now shifting my focus toward user accessibility and the default experience. I'm going to set the default theme to "light" for broader appeal and remove the admin restriction to allow everyone to upload, edit, and delete projects. This will be easier through direct access in the menu bar. I plan to modify the `App.tsx` file for this implementation.


**Adding Upload Functionality**

I'm now prioritizing the new "Upload" functionality in the navigation bar. My current plan is to add an "Upload" button to the navbar that, when clicked, scrolls the user directly to the projects section. The goal is to make the "Add New" modal easily accessible. I'm considering passing a prop `openAddModal` to the nav component and triggering the modal opening from the navbar if possible to streamline the process. The simpler fallback, though, is to link to the projects section, letting the current "Add Project" button handle the modal.


**Simplifying Upload Controls**

I'm now streamlining the admin controls for project uploads. Instead of separate upload functionality, I'm making the existing admin buttons consistently visible and considering an added link to the projects section. My aim is to make the process more straightforward for the user. I plan on making the `isAdmin` value default to true to allow those buttons, and if necessary, I'll add a specific "Upload" navigation item that scrolls the user directly to the `#projects` section.

**Implementing Interactive Particles**

I'm now focusing on the hero animation, specifically `components/Visuals.tsx`. I will replace or augment the existing `Canvas` with a more interactive particle system as requested. I'm exploring `@react-three/drei`'s `Sparkles` or creating a custom `Points` mesh that reacts to mouse position. I need to figure out how to best handle mouse position input, either by passing it to the canvas or handling it directly within the canvas using `useThree` and event listeners.


**Updating Admin Functionality**

I'm now refactoring admin controls with `useState(true)` for `isAdmin`. I'll add "Upload" to navigation links. Additionally, I'll modify the `ProjectSystem.tsx` to ensure the "Add Project" button is readily visible, as `isAdmin` will always be true. The focus is on simplifying the upload process. I'll create an `InteractiveParticles` component in `Visuals.tsx` using `useFrame` to update particle positions based on a mouse pointer vector. Finally, I'll set the default theme to 'light' in `App.tsx` and make the admin default to true. An "Upload" button will be added to the nav.


**Refining Modal Control**

I'm now zeroing in on managing the project modal state. My current approach involves creating `showAddModal` and `setShowAddModal` within `App.tsx` and passing these to `ProjectSystem`. I'll modify `ProjectSystem` to respond to the `showAddModal` trigger. My plan is to simplify user interactions, so I'll set `isAdmin` to `true` and add a straightforward "Upload Work" button in the Navbar.


**Refining Modal Integration**

I'm now integrating the modal directly within the project system component. I'm focusing on passing `isUploadModalOpen` and `setUploadModalOpen` as props from the app to the `ProjectSystem.tsx` file. When `isUploadOpen` is true, it displays the modal, mirroring the existing "edit" functionality. I'm also considering refactoring `ProjectSystem` to lift the modal state or just adding a ref to streamline things. Alternatively, I can simplify by always enabling `isAdmin` which means the button will always be visible.


**Implementing Upload Trigger**

I'm now focused on triggering the modal from the navbar. My plan involves `App.tsx` handling the trigger state, setting `addProjectTrigger` to `true` when the "Upload" button is clicked and scrolling to the projects section, then passing this state to `ProjectSystem`. `ProjectSystem` will use a `useEffect` to respond to this trigger and open the modal, calling a callback to reset the trigger after it opens. I'll also ensure `isAdmin` is always true. I'll implement interactive particles in `Visuals.tsx` using `useFrame`.


**Implementing Particle Swarm**

I'm now diving into the code. I'm building an interactive particle swarm in `Visuals.tsx`. I'll set the default theme to "light" in `App.tsx` and ensure everyone has admin rights, simplifying `isAdmin` to always be true. The navbar will get an "Upload" button to trigger the project upload. My core focus is the interactivity of the new visuals.


**Developing Interactive Particles**

I'm now integrating an interactive particle swarm within `Visuals.tsx` using `useFrame`. I'll set a 'light' default theme and ensure everyone has admin access, simplifying isAdmin to always be true. The navbar will get an "Upload" button. My focus is the interactivity of the new visuals.


**Refining Particle Interactivity**

I'm now iterating on interactive particles, currently implementing a `MeshTransmissionMaterial` torus in `Visuals.tsx`. The goal is creating a responsive particle system. I'm focusing on the `useFrame` hook to update particle positions based on mouse movement. I'll need to figure out best how to map mouse coordinates to 3D space.


**Implementing Particle Mouse Interaction**

I'm now making the particles interactive based on mouse position. I'm interpolating particle positions towards the mouse coordinates within the `useFrame` loop in `Visuals.tsx`. I'm adjusting the x and y offsets to achieve an attractive particle movement effect. I also added some random factor to the z coordinate to give depth. My focus is achieving a visually engaging response.


**Optimizing Particle Movement**

I'm now refining the particle movement within the `useFrame` loop of `Visuals.tsx`. I'm calculating and applying more dynamic forces based on mouse coordinates, with `xOffset` and `yOffset` to create engaging movements. I've also incorporated random factors to the z-coordinate for added depth. The goal is to achieve a visually attractive and reactive particle system.



**Refining Light System**

I'm now integrating lights and visuals with the default theme. I added a `pointLight` that changes color based on `isLight`. I've added a few `FloatingLens` components. The lights are now more dynamic and responsive. I've also swapped out simple shapes for interactive particles. I am focusing on the balance between visual appeal and performance.



**Integrating Project Data**

I'm now integrating the mock project data. I'm focusing on rendering this data in a grid layout with card components. I'll utilize Framer Motion to animate the transitions between projects and implement Drag and Drop to allow for reordering the projects.
I will also need to figure out how to handle the different project details, modal updates, and other interactions when the project data gets bigger.


**Rendering Project Data**

I'm now focused on rendering the project data. I'm working on displaying the project data in a grid layout using card components and incorporating Framer Motion for smooth transitions. I'm prioritizing the display of project details and will handle user interactions as the data grows in size. The goal is a visually appealing and responsive display.


**Refining Project Interaction**

I'm now implementing project interactivity, focusing on edit and delete functions with `ProjectSystem`. I'm using `useState` for `setSelectedProject`, `isEditing`, and `editForm` to manage project states. I'm incorporating a file input ref and ensuring light theme behavior with the `isLight` state.  I'm also setting up `useEffect` to respond to external add project triggers.


**Implementing Project Controls**

I'm now integrating edit, save, add, and delete controls within `ProjectSystem`. I'm using `useState` hooks for `selectedProject`, `isEditing`, and `editForm` to manage project states. I'm focusing on file uploads, implementing the `handleFileUpload` function with a `FileReader` for image previews. My focus is streamlined project management. I'm also ensuring light theme behavior and responsive external trigger handling using `useEffect`.


**Organizing Project Display**

I'm now arranging and filtering project components based on user selection. I'm focusing on dynamic rendering of projects, enabling 'Edit' and 'Delete' functions for project management. I'm handling the data displayed in the card components. I am prioritizing a smooth user experience.


**Refining Project Interactions**

I'm now iterating on the `ProjectSystem`, adding interactions to display projects. I'm focusing on edit, save, add, and delete controls within a card component. I'll make the `handleFileUpload` function utilize the `FileReader` for image previews. My aim is simple project management. I'm also ensuring consistent light theme behavior and responsive triggers for easy access.


**Refining Project Card**

I'm now refining individual project card displays using Framer Motion. I'm focusing on hover animations, applying `whileHover` to create a subtle vertical lift. Additionally, I am including `transition-transform` and `scale-110` effects for the video hover states. I'm optimizing this implementation for improved visual engagement and responsiveness.


**Optimizing Project Rendering**

I'm now optimizing project rendering. I'm focusing on hover animations within project cards using Framer Motion. I've implemented a vertical lift on hover, as well as `transition-transform` and `scale-110` effects for the video states. I'm focusing on enhanced visual engagement with a smooth user experience.


**Implementing Card Overlay**

I'm now integrating an admin overlay for project cards. The plan is to create edit and delete buttons that appear on hover. I'm utilizing `group-hover` and `transition-opacity` for a smooth transition. The focus is to allow consistent visibility to those controls. I've placed the buttons at the top right for a clean design.


**Refining Project Modal**

I'm now working on the modal details view for each project. I'm focusing on responsiveness and visual consistency. I'm using `AnimatePresence` and `motion.div` for smooth transitions. I'm also implementing a dark/light mode toggle to ensure good contrast.


**Improving Modal Display**

I'm now focusing on improving the project details modal. I added a close button in the top right. I'm prioritizing modal visual consistency and responsive design with `AnimatePresence`, `motion.div`, and dark/light mode toggling for contrast. I aim to create a clean, user-friendly presentation.


**Refining Project Content**

I'm now populating the modal with project details. I've integrated project data like category, title, description, and client deliverables into the modal view. I'm focusing on ensuring consistent text styling and responsiveness, using tailwind classes. I'm making sure it adjusts dynamically to dark/light theme.


**Enhancing Project Modals**

I'm now focusing on refining the project modal's display and functionality. I added a close button and am refining dark/light mode integration using dynamic styling. I'm prioritizing user experience with a clean, responsive modal, with dynamic content rendering and responsive styling.


**Refining Form Fields**

I'm now refining form fields within the project modal to enhance the user experience. I'm focusing on text inputs and select elements for category selection. I'm adjusting their styling to provide a visual distinction for both light and dark themes. I'm ensuring all inputs are easily readable.


**Integrating File Upload**

I'm now integrating file upload functionality into the project modal. I've added a file input with a ref. I'll utilize a `FileReader` in `handleFileUpload` to generate image previews. I'm focusing on user-friendly upload interaction.


**Finalizing Modal Interaction**

I'm now implementing file upload integration for project modals. I've added a file input with a ref, utilizing `FileReader` in the `handleFileUpload` function. My current focus is to provide a user-friendly and visually clear file upload experience.


**Finalizing Upload Functionality**

I'm now putting the finishing touches on the project upload functionality and working on the project details page.  I've created and styled text inputs, and category select elements, making sure they're visually distinct in both light and dark themes. The goal is to provide a user-friendly and visually appealing upload interface.


**Finalizing Component Integration**

I'm now integrating all components. I've finished implementing the modal with file upload and project details. I've focused on ensuring a responsive and intuitive design. I'm now polishing the overall user experience and visual consistency across the entire application.


**Integrating Project Display**

I'm now integrating the rendering of project display, with a strong focus on display and interaction. The modal is now finished, with styled form fields and integrated file upload. The goal is now a user-friendly and fully functional display. I'm focusing on ensuring visual consistency, and responsive behavior within the display.


**Finalizing Application Integration**

I'm now putting together the final touches, bringing the complete application into focus. The modal, with all components, is complete with refined visual consistency and responsive design. My final focus is on the user experience and ensuring seamless behavior across all sections, integrating the modal.


**Finalizing Integration and Polish**

I am now focusing on the final tweaks to create a high-quality experience. I've focused on ensuring the visual elements, like the gradient overlay with the glow theme, are displaying as expected. I'm focusing on responsiveness and smooth navigation, ensuring seamless behavior across the entire application and complete user-friendliness.


**Refining Theme Integration**

I'm now integrating theme toggling within the navigation bar, ensuring smooth transitions and visual consistency with the light and dark modes. I'm focusing on crafting the 'Upload' button style within the navigation bar, while also ensuring all elements adapt responsively to theme changes. I'm working on visual details, and refining overall user-friendliness.


**Implementing Theme Buttons**

I'm now integrating the theme toggling buttons within the navigation bar, specifically the sun, moon, and sparkles icons. I'm focusing on their visual styling and responsiveness, making sure they function properly. I'll need to link them to update the `theme` state and ensure consistent appearance across themes. I am focusing on seamless user theme selection.


**Implementing Theme Buttons**

I'm now focusing on implementing the theme toggle within the navigation. I'm focusing on user-friendliness by designing theme toggle functionality. I'll need to update the `theme` state and ensure consistent visual appearance across themes. I'm focusing on the integration of the toggle functionality to match the design.


**Implementing Navigation Bar**

I'm now integrating the navigation functionality with theme toggle implementation. I've added theme toggle functionality to the navigation bar. I am adding an "Upload Project" button that, when clicked, sets the `triggerAddProject` state to true.

The portfolio website has been updated to include the requested enhancements:

1.  **Immersive Hero Background**: The `BackgroundScene` now includes an `InteractiveParticles` system. This generates a field of 150 floating particles that subtly react to mouse movement (using `useFrame` and vector interpolation) for a cinematic, fluid feel.

2.  **Default Light Mode**: The application now defaults to `'light'` mode on load.

3.  **Universal Upload Access**:
    *   **Admin Mode Enabled**: All users now have access to "Admin" features (adding, editing, deleting projects) by default.
    *   **Navbar Upload Option**: A dedicated "Upload" button has been added to the main navigation bar (and mobile menu).
    *   **Direct Interaction**: Clicking "Upload" in the menu automatically scrolls to the Projects section and opens the "Add New Project" modal.

4.  **UI Refinements**: Adjusted lighting intensities in the 3D scene to ensure the new particle system looks great in the default light mode.

These changes make the site more interactive and ensure the "upload" workflow is front-and-center for all users.

In the 'New Project' form within the Project System, add a button next to the 'Image / Video URL' input field. This button should be labeled 'Upload Media' and, when clicked, trigger the file input dialog to allow users to upload an image or video file.

**Initiating the Analysis**

I'm currently dissecting the task. The core goal is adding an "Upload Media" button beside the existing input field within the specified file. This button should launch a file selection dialog. It's a focused UI enhancement within the `ProjectSystem.tsx` component. I'm visualizing the integration now.


**Refining the UI Element**

I'm now focusing on the UI element itself. The provided code already *has* an upload button. My task involves modifying it to satisfy the user's specific request for a "labeled 'Upload Media'" button. I'm considering whether to change the existing icon-only button to a text-labeled button, or add a second button with the label, and will consider the context for the UI design. I'm leaning toward modification of the existing button.


**Implementing the Text Label**

I've decided to incorporate the text label directly into the existing button. I'm focusing on the code modification to include "Upload Media" alongside the existing icon. I'm also ensuring the visual design remains consistent with the current style, paying attention to the spacing and alignment of the text with the icon. The change involves adding a `<span>` element within the button to render the desired label, and I'm adding `flex items-center gap-2` to the button to manage the icon and text alignment.


**Addressing the Light Mode**

I am now addressing the light mode and dynamic background issues. I've reviewed `App.tsx` and updated the theme state to default to 'light' instead of 'midnight'. Furthermore, I've checked `components/Visuals.tsx` and have identified the need to integrate `InteractiveParticles` for the animated background. Finally, I'm noting the need to allow *all* users to upload their projects by adjusting the `isAdmin` state.


**Reconciling Prompt Conflicts**

I'm now clarifying the scope, as there's a discrepancy in prompts. The current focus is the "Upload Media" button in the "New Project" form; the menu bar option might have been a separate request. I'll ensure I address *this* immediate prompt specifically, and reconcile the others later, though I see `isAdmin` needs to be addressed for the broader UI concerns. I'll consider defaulting this to `true` or removing it for this specific feature.


**Synthesizing Requirements**

I'm now integrating all requirements, recognizing discrepancies. The provided file snapshot reflects an *old* state, lacking the desired particles, default light mode, and navbar upload button. My plan will incorporate all requests, including the "Upload Media" label, the default 'light' theme, the interactive background, and the universal upload access/navbar upload button. I'll pass a `triggerAddProject` prop to `ProjectSystem` to keep things simple.


**Clarifying Component Interaction**

I'm now focusing on how the menu bar upload interacts with the `ProjectSystem`. I've realized `ProjectSystem` contains the modal state and the upload button. The key is allowing users to *trigger* the modal from the menu bar. I've considered several methods, and will pass an `openUpload` prop. I also realize `isAdmin` currently restricts upload access, so I'll set it to `true` by default, as the user wants all to upload. I'll test this thoroughly.


**Expanding the Functionality**

I'm now incorporating changes to the `App.tsx` file to address the navbar upload option. I'm adding a new "Upload" button to the Desktop Nav and Mobile Menu that scrolls to `#projects`, which the user requested. I'm keeping the `isAdmin` logic, but defaulting it to `true` to ensure all users can see the controls. I will also incorporate the `InteractiveParticles` component into `components/Visuals.tsx`. The implementation of particles will use `instancedMesh` or simple points that react to the mouse pointer. I'll test each of these separately for clarity.


**Integrating the Upload Logic**

I'm now focusing on connecting the Navbar "Upload" button to the existing upload functionality. I've decided to add an "Upload Work" button to the Navbar, which will scroll the user to the `#projects` section. This will leverage the existing "Add Project" button that's available due to the `isAdmin` state being set to `true`. This solution fulfills the user's request for a menu bar upload option, tying the global upload button to the upload feature.


**Prioritizing the Update**

I'm now zeroing in on the "New Project" upload button. I've re-examined the code, confirming the existing button. I'll focus on the modifications here, specifically to add the "Upload Media" text to the button, as well as fixing the visuals. In the provided snippet, the `Upload` button now has the text, and I'll keep the particles and defaults the user has requested.


**Implementing the Changes**

I'm now implementing the changes to include a particles background in `components/Visuals.tsx`. I'll rewrite the component, and consider `drei`'s `Instances` or just raw `points`. The `drei` `Stars` component exists, but is static, so I will add a custom `Particles` component. Next, I'll update `App.tsx` and change the `useState` defaults. Finally, I'll return to `components/ProjectSystem.tsx` to update the button label as planned.

**Refining Project Interaction**

I'm currently focused on the `ProjectSystem` component. The key change I'm exploring is how project thumbnails behave. Instead of launching a modal when a thumbnail is clicked, the intent is to have it redirect directly to the URL that is currently stored in the `image` field. This change aims to streamline the user experience when interacting with projects.


**Clarifying Data Structure**

I'm now wrestling with the `Project` data structure, specifically how to represent project URLs. The current assumption is the user wants a dedicated `link` field, and the existing `image` field is specifically for media display. I'm reviewing the "Add Project" form to ensure it aligns with this new field. I am considering the nuances of image vs. external URLs to make sure this is the most useful data structure.


**Defining Click Interaction**

I've zeroed in on the click behavior. The goal is to open the attached URL when a project card is clicked, necessitating a `link` property in the `Project` interface. I'm updating the "Add/Edit Project" modal to include a corresponding input field for the URL. I'm also considering automatic thumbnail generation from video URLs or uploads.


**Updating Click Behavior**

I've determined the approach for clicking project cards. I'll utilize `window.open(project.link, '_blank')` within the `onClick` handler. Additionally, I am removing the now-unnecessary "Detail Modal" logic and corresponding state management, as the user will be redirected to the project URL directly. I am also investigating whether or not to include a dedicated `thumbnail` field to the `Project` interface, or if the existing `image` field will do.


**Analyzing Image-Video Interaction**

I'm now contemplating the user's intent concerning video thumbnails. The existing hover-to-play functionality creates a conflict if a static poster is desired. I'm reassessing how `isVideo` logic handles the `image` field, considering it as a potential thumbnail holder. I'm exploring the implications of allowing video file uploads, and debating the value of auto-generating thumbnails from videos. The current architecture allows a video to be uploaded to the `image` field, so I'm investigating how this will work with the project URL field.


**Implementing Link Functionality**

I'm now implementing the `link` property in `types.ts` and updating `INITIAL_PROJECTS` with dummy URLs. The "Project Link" input is being added to the form, with the click handler updated to open `project.link` in a new tab. I am focusing on streamlining the video thumbnail logic with the changes, I will use a video data URL to generate the thumbnail if a video is uploaded.


**Automating Thumbnail Generation**

I'm now determining the interplay between video uploads and thumbnail generation. If I detect a video upload, I'll automatically extract the first frame to use as the `image` thumbnail. I'm also ensuring the existing `isVideo` logic handles this scenario correctly, and investigating how to best preserve the "hover-to-play" video functionality while also generating thumbnails. This ensures the best user experience.


**Simplifying Field Logic**

I'm now zeroing in on the video thumbnail and `image` field interaction. I'll maintain the current system where the `image` field can be either a video or an image. The user's request is interpreted as automatic thumbnail generation from a video URL or upload. Therefore, I will automatically extract the first frame as the thumbnail image from uploaded video, and assign it to the image field. This keeps the data structure streamlined.


**Refining Upload Behavior**

I'm now determining when to automatically generate the thumbnail. When the user uploads a video file, I will extract a thumbnail and replace the existing data in the `image` field. This streamlines the user experience and satisfies the user's intent to view a static thumbnail and go to a URL. This minimizes heavy page loading and still addresses the user's requirements.


**Resolving Video Thumbnail Issue**

I'm now grappling with the video thumbnail dilemma. If I replace the video with a static image, the "hover-to-play" feature disappears. My current intent is to extract a static thumbnail and assign it to the `image` field when the user uploads a video. The click behavior is to go to the assigned URL. The existing `isVideo` function will return false after this upload. I'm exploring if this is an acceptable tradeoff.


**Reconciling Video Logic**

I'm now integrating the thumbnail generation into the upload handling, addressing the conflicting video preview concern. If a video is uploaded, I'll extract a thumbnail, update the `editForm.image` with the thumbnail's data URL, and remove the video from the upload. This is a compromise; it sacrifices the hover-to-play preview for a static thumbnail, while preserving the intent to open the attached URL.


**Implementing Video Thumbnail**

I'm now prioritizing the automatic thumbnail generation for videos on upload, and the introduction of a dedicated `link` field for project URLs. I'll automatically extract the first frame from uploaded videos and set this as the `image` thumbnail to align with the "add thumbnail automatically" direction. I am making updates to both the `types.ts` and `ProjectSystem.tsx` to accommodate the `link` field and implement the automatic thumbnail extraction during video uploads. I'll maintain the `isVideo` check for URLs.


**Integrating Thumbnail Logic**

I'm now integrating the thumbnail generation logic directly into the `handleFileUpload` function. This leverages the `generateThumbnail` helper to extract a frame from uploaded videos. Simultaneously, I'm updating `INITIAL_PROJECTS` with placeholder URLs to test the new functionality. I'm focusing on ensuring these changes harmonize smoothly with the existing type definitions in `types.ts`.


**Implementing Project Updates**

I'm now fully immersed in updating the codebase. I've added the `link` property to the `Project` interface in `types.ts` and updated `INITIAL_PROJECTS` with sample URLs. I'm removing the modal logic from `ProjectSystem.tsx` and updating the click handler to redirect to the project link. I'm now integrating the data and testing the changes to ensure everything aligns as intended.

To implement the requested changes, I will update `types.ts` to include a `link` property for projects, and modify `components/ProjectSystem.tsx` to handle the new navigation behavior and automatic video thumbnail generation.

### Changes Implemented:

1.  **`types.ts`**: Added `link?: string` to the `Project` interface.
2.  **`components/ProjectSystem.tsx`**:
    *   **Project Navigation**: Changed the `onClick` handler for project cards to open the `project.link` in a new tab, removing the previous detail modal functionality.
    *   **Link Input**: Added a "Project Link" input field to the Add/Edit form.
    *   **Auto-Thumbnail Generation**: Implemented logic in the file upload handler. If a video file is selected, it creates a hidden video element, seeks to the 1-second mark, captures a frame on a canvas, and sets that image as the project thumbnail. This replaces the heavy video file with a lightweight static image for the grid.

entries.description: Add link property to Project interface.
Implement click-to-link behavior and auto-thumbnail generation from video uploads.

payload.text: Now the delete button is not working the after clicking the delete button the video is not removing from my app or website make it function after after clicking the delete button it should be fully permanently remove the from my app

**Analyzing Delete Functionality**

I'm currently focused on the `handleDelete` function within the `ProjectSystem.tsx` component. The delete button isn't removing projects, so I'm diving into that function to understand the failure point. I'm going to check the code that should be removing the object.


**Investigating Persistence Concerns**

I've examined the `handleDelete` function in `ProjectSystem.tsx` and the state update logic seems correct for React. However, I realize my initial assessment was incomplete. The current implementation only affects the in-memory state. I need to consider persistence and how the initial projects are loaded.  My current thought is that this is not permanently removing the project from the app. I think the user may be expecting this.


**Revisiting Delete Functionality**

I'm circling back to `handleDelete` in `ProjectSystem.tsx`. The core issue, confirmed by user feedback, is that items aren't actually being removed from the display. I'm focusing on how `project.id` is being utilized to filter and update the `projects` state. I am checking again for any potential issues with state updates or closures, but haven't found any. My current focus is how the `projects` state is updated within the `handleDelete` function to ensure it reflects the intended changes.


**Refining Delete Logic**

I'm now zeroing in on the best way to implement the delete functionality. I realized the `handleDelete` function might be capturing an outdated `projects` variable due to its closure over the render. I'm exploring the functional update approach for `setProjects` to guarantee accurate state updates. Also, it's clear I need to address the persistence issue, since this is a client-side app, so I'm thinking of using `localStorage` to save the deleted state.


**Implementing Persistence Solutions**

I am now focused on making sure the projects persist between page reloads, which is important for the user. I've broken this into implementation tasks. First, I need to update the `ProjectSystem.tsx` component. Next, I must initialize the project state from `localStorage` if it is present; if not, then the app will use `INITIAL_PROJECTS`. Then I'll save `projects` to `localStorage` whenever they change, using `useEffect`. Finally, I'll update the `handleDelete` method to use functional state updates. I also realized I needed to import `useEffect`, and updated the import statements.


**Confirming Project Persistence**

I've implemented the crucial persistence aspect using `localStorage`. The project list now correctly initializes from saved data or the default list. I'm leveraging `useEffect` to ensure `localStorage` stays updated whenever `projects` change, including in the `handleDelete` method. I've updated the `handleDelete` function to utilize functional state updates, ensuring it's always working with the current state. I've also verified `handleEdit` and `handleSave` will save to local storage. I'm now verifying the file contents.


**Finalizing State Management**

I'm now integrating the final pieces for robust state management and persistence within the `ProjectSystem` component. I've focused on `useEffect` to manage the lifecycle of project data within `localStorage`, ensuring consistent updates and proper initialization. `handleDelete` is updated to fully leverage this, using a functional state update to guarantee that projects are deleted accurately. I'm also ensuring that event propagation is halted in `handleDelete` to prevent issues. All aspects of the state lifecycle are now handled.


**Addressing Edge Cases**

I'm now focusing on a potential edge case: corrupted data in `localStorage`. To handle this, I've added a `try-catch` block within the `useState` initializer to gracefully handle invalid JSON. My goal is to ensure the application doesn't crash if the user has tampered with the stored data. I've also incorporated functional state updates for the delete method.


**Refactoring Save Operations**

I'm now revising the `handleSave` function to improve its resilience. I am changing the logic to consistently use functional updates for the state, which avoids potential stale closure issues. This ensures the correct state is always being used to update the projects. This also ensures that the updates to local storage, implemented previously, are up-to-date and consistent.

To address the issue where deleted projects are not being removed permanently (likely reappearing on refresh) and to ensure the delete button functions reliably, I will update the `ProjectSystem` component to use **Local Storage**. This creates a persistent data store in your browser, so changes like deletions, edits, and new uploads are saved "permanently" across page reloads.

### Changes Implemented:

1.  **Local Storage Persistence**:
    *   Updated the `projects` state initialization to check `localStorage` first. If saved data exists, it loads that; otherwise, it loads the default `INITIAL_PROJECTS`.
    *   Added a `useEffect` hook that automatically saves the `projects` list to `localStorage` whenever you add, edit, or delete a project.
2.  **Robust Delete Function**:
    *   Updated `handleDelete` to use the functional state update pattern (`setProjects(prev => ...)`). This ensures it always operates on the most current data, preventing bugs where the UI might not reflect the deletion immediately.
    *   Added a confirmation dialog to prevent accidental deletions.

entries.description: Implement Local Storage persistence for permanent deletion/editing and update delete handler logic.
